_meta.notes: All slots and attributes verified directly from NPM package source. Do not re-derive for known components — use this registry.

header-utility.label: Global University Header
header-utility.notes: Fixed UMD global bar — identical on every page. Self-contained: renders UMD flag, 'A Top 20 Public Research University', and utility nav. No slots, no attrs needed. Hardcode in every template. Never touch during page updates.

header-site.label: Site Navigation Header

slots.name: logo
slots.description: Anchor tag wrapping a logo image or text. Short text (<30 chars) triggers large logo sizing automatically.

slots.name: main-navigation
slots.description: Container holding umd-element-nav-item children. Component extracts nav items automatically.

slots.name: utility-navigation
slots.description: Optional utility row shown above the nav row on desktop.

attrs.name: cta-url
attrs.description: CTA button URL — requires cta-text to activate

attrs.name: cta-text
attrs.description: CTA button label — requires cta-url to activate

attrs.name: sticky
attrs.description: Triggers sticky behavior when set to 'true'

variants.sticky: Add sticky attribute for fixed positioning on scroll

header-site.notes: Site-level header — logo and nav links change per site. The component auto-registers umd-element-nav-item children from the main-navigation slot. CTA button only appears when both cta-url and cta-text are present.

nav-item.label: Navigation Item

slots.name: primary-link
slots.description: The main anchor link for this nav item.

slots.name: dropdown-links
slots.description: Sub-navigation links that appear in the dropdown. Use a <div> containing bare <a> elements — one per dropdown item. This is the primary slot for standard nav dropdowns.
slots.example: Meet the Dean College Administration Giving

slots.name: dropdown-callout
slots.description: Optional rich callout panel shown alongside dropdown-links. Use for featured content, images, or CTAs within the dropdown. Children must have content to render.

nav-item.notes: Used inside the main-navigation slot of umd-element-navigation-header. Each nav link = one umd-element-nav-item. For standard dropdowns with a list of links, use slot="dropdown-links" with bare <a> children — this is what generates the dropdown list. slot="dropdown-callout" is for rich callout content alongside the links and only renders if its children have content.

nav-slider.label: Navigation Slider
nav-slider.description: Left-side navigation panel for interior pages. Displays primary nav links (with optional secondary/action links) in a collapsible drawer on mobile and a 280px fixed column on desktop. Requires an interior layout CSS grid wrapper — the component does not handle its own column positioning.

slots.name: primary-slide-links
slots.description: Parent section link(s). Use a <div> with bare <a> elements — each <a> is a section label displayed BOLD. For interior page sidebars, this is just the parent section (e.g. 'About UMD'). Add data-child-ref="{id}" on the <a> to wire it to a children-slides panel with matching data-parent-ref.

slots.name: children-slides
slots.description: Sub-panel containing child page links at normal (non-bold) weight. Use a <div slot='children-slides'> containing one or more <div data-parent-ref='{id}'> groups. The data-parent-ref must match the data-child-ref on the parent link. Add data-active to the div that should be shown on load (the current section). Mark the current page link with data-selected. This pattern also triggers the 'back' indicator automatically.
slots.example: History & Traditions Traditions of the Past University Symbols

slots.name: primary-slide-secondary-links
slots.description: Secondary action links displayed below the primary nav links (e.g. Apply Now, Request Info). Use a <div> with bare <a> children.

slots.name: primary-slide-content
slots.description: Optional custom content block rendered in the primary slide panel.

attrs.name: resize
attrs.description: Set to 'true' to trigger recalculation of slider dimensions. Use when the component's container size changes after initial render (e.g. on window resize or dynamic content updates).

nav-slider.critical_css: umd-element-nav-slider requires container-type: inline-size in critical CSS (same group as umd-element-hero-minimal and umd-element-footer).
nav-slider.layout_note: The component does not position itself. Wrap it in an interior-layout grid alongside <main> content. At ≥1024px the grid is 280px (nav) + 1fr (content) with a 48px gap. On mobile/tablet both columns stack. Apply umd-layout-space-horizontal-larger (or -normal) on the outer wrapper.
nav-slider.layout_css: .interior-layout { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr; gap: 0; align-items: start; } @media (min-width: 1024px) { .interior-layout { grid-template-columns: 280px 1fr; gap: 48px; } }

side nav.description: Alias for nav slider — interior page left navigation

nav-slider.notes: Two-level navigation pattern: primary-slide-links holds the bold section header(s); children-slides holds the child page links at normal weight with the back indicator. Use data-child-ref on parent <a> and matching data-parent-ref on the children <div>. Add data-active to the currently open children panel. Add data-selected to the current page link. Wrap all <a> text in <span>. Use <div> (not <nav>) for slot containers.
nav-slider.example:
About UMD
History & Traditions Traditions of the Past University Symbols Fight Song

footer.label: Footer
footer.description: University-branded site footer. Three display variants: simple (default), mega (three nav columns), and visual (image background). Automatically sets role='contentinfo' and aria-label='site footer'. The global utility header (umd-element-utility-header) is always at the top of the page; this footer closes the page.

slots.name: logo
slots.description: Department/unit logo. Must be an element with an inside.

slots.name: contact-headline
slots.description: Heading for the contact/address block.

slots.name: address
slots.description: Mailing address block.

slots.name: contact-links
slots.description: Contact links (email, phone) as a list or inline links.

slots.name: social-links
slots.description: Social media links. Use a <ul> of <li><a> items.

slots.name: utility-links
slots.description: Utility/legal nav links (Privacy Policy, Web Accessibility, etc). Use a <ul>.

slots.name: actions
slots.description: Primary CTA button displayed prominently in the footer.

slots.name: link-column-one
slots.description: First nav column. Mega footer only. Use a <nav> with a <ul> of links.

slots.name: link-column-two
slots.description: Second nav column. Mega footer only.

slots.name: link-column-three
slots.description: Third nav column. Mega footer only.

slots.name: image
slots.description: Background image. Visual footer only.

attrs.name: data-display
attrs.description: Footer variant. Omit or use 'simple' for default. 'mega' enables three nav columns. 'visual' uses a background image.

notes.simple: Default single-column footer with contact info and social links
notes.mega: Full footer with up to three nav link columns, contact, and social
notes.visual: Image-backed footer — set slot='image' for background

attrs.name: data-theme
attrs.description: Light theme — dark text on light gray background instead of dark footer.

footer mega.description: Mega footer with three nav columns

footer light.description: Footer with light theme (dark text on light background)

footer.notes:
role='contentinfo' and aria-label='site footer' are auto-applied if absent. Logo slot must be an wrapping an . Link columns only render if they have child nodes. Simple is default if neither mega nor visual is set. CRITICAL: umd-element-footer uses extract-and-append (not slot projection) — slot content is moved into the shadow DOM. Light-DOM CSS targeting [slot='address'] has no effect. The shadow DOM applies color:white only to p, a, and span children. Wrap each address line in a — raw text nodes and
tags render as black text on the dark footer background.
footer.example:
College of Engineering

1101 Engineering BuildingCollege Park, MD 20742
eng@umd.edu (301) 405-1000

  • Facebook
  • Privacy Policy
  • Web Accessibility
